duration.note: Except by permission of Senate, a student shall be required to complete the equivalent of 72 credits per year of study.

registration_limits.note: Students failing to meet readmission requirements (FB5.1-FB5.3) may be excluded. Senate may grant permission to continue with fewer credits.

FB7.1.note: All compulsory cross-faculty courses count as Science credits per FB7.6 Note 2 (e.g. EEE for Computer Engineering, HUB for Human Anatomy & Physiology, INF for Business Computing).

options.description: At least one Science Mathematics course of 18 NQF credits (level 5) AND one Science Statistics course of 18 NQF credits (level 5).
Science Mathematics courses totalling 36 NQF credits (level 5) — no separate Statistics course required.

FB7.4.free_replacement_note: Up to 72 Science credits may be freely replaced by non-Science faculty courses (subject to exclusions and approval by a Student Advisor or Deputy Dean).
FB7.4.hierarchical_sequence_note: Where more than 72 Science credits are replaced, each additional non-Science course must form part of a hierarchical sequence linked to those already completed.
FB7.4.excluded_courses_note: Courses taught by the Faculty of Science for other faculties are not available to Science-registered students. Students transferring into Science from other faculties may count such courses with Faculty approval.

cross_faculty_routes_model.purpose: Defines structured cross-faculty elective sequences for Science students where approved pathways align with second or third major intentions in other faculties.

FB7.5.description: Major(s) requirement — at least one Science major required
restrictions: Business Computing may only be taken in conjunction with a major in Computer Science.
Computer Engineering may only be taken in conjunction with a major in Computer Science.
Applied Statistics, Mathematical Statistics, and Statistics & Data Science may not be taken together.
FB7.5.entry_note: Acceptance into the Science Faculty does not guarantee acceptance into a chosen major. Formal acceptance for specific majors (currently Biochemistry, Genetics, and Human Anatomy & Physiology) is limited by student numbers and takes place at the start of the second year.

FB7.6.note_1: Compulsory courses listed per major are the minimum required in addition to those in FB7.3.
FB7.6.note_2: All courses taught in other Faculties that are required/compulsory for a Science major count as Science credits for FB7.1 (e.g. EEE courses for Computer Engineering, HUB courses for Human Anatomy & Physiology, INF courses for Business Computing).
FB7.6.note_3: Faculty-approved equivalent courses may be substituted. Common equivalences: BIO1000H ≡ BIO1000F; CSC1010H ≡ CSC1015F/S; CSC1011H ≡ CSC1016S; MAM1004F/S (≥65%) ≡ MAM1031F; MAM1005H ≡ MAM1031/3F; MAM1006H ≡ MAM1032/4S; PHY1023H ≡ PHY1031F; (CEM1009H+CEM1010H) ≡ CEM1000W.

FB7.7.note: The curriculum must contain at least 72 credits of distinct level-7 courses recognised by the Faculty for each major. With Deputy Dean approval, up to 36 credits may count towards more than one major. Rules FB7.1-FB7.3 must still be met independently.

course_code_system.format: 2–4 letter department prefix + 4-digit number + semester indicator

semester_indicators.H: Half course (semester, used in some programmes)
semester_indicators.X: Year-long course spanning both semesters but assessed separately

essential_terminology.DP: Duly Performed — a certificate required to sit for final examinations, earned by meeting minimum attendance and engagement requirements during the year.
essential_terminology.NQF: National Qualifications Framework — a standardised credit and level system for South African education.
essential_terminology.half_course: A course worth approximately 18 NQF credits, typically spanning one semester.
essential_terminology.full_year_course: A course worth approximately 36 NQF credits, spanning both semesters.
essential_terminology.science_credit: Any NQF credit from a Science Faculty course, or from a cross-faculty course designated as Science-equivalent under FB7.6 Note 2.
essential_terminology.hierarchical_sequence: A series of courses where later courses build on and require the successful completion of earlier courses in the sequence.
essential_terminology.supplementary_examination: An additional examination offered to students who narrowly fail (typically 45–49%), subject to Faculty rules.

progression_status_codes.GS: Good Standing — student meets all progression criteria and may re-register without restriction.
progression_status_codes.RS: Restricted Standing — student may re-register with conditions imposed by the Faculty.
progression_status_codes.FC: Faculty Concession — special permission granted by Faculty to register under non-standard conditions.
progression_status_codes.EX: Excluded — student does not meet re-registration requirements and is not permitted to re-register.
